title: lupinus cusickii subsp. abortivus
intro: Lupinus cusickii subsp. abortivus, also known as Cusick's lupine, is a subspecies of lupine, a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lupinus cusickii subsp. abortivus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in herbal supplements or as a topical application, but proper identification and preparation are crucial to ensure safe use.
